---
Call the seo-manager MCP tool `get_instructions` with workflow `backlinks` and follow the returned markdown exactly. Target: $ARGUMENTS

---

---
Call the seo-manager MCP tool `get_instructions` with workflow `build-tool` and follow the returned markdown exactly (it is the current playbook; also read `.dispatchseo/conventions.md` for this repo's site facts). Tools ONLY - type="tool". If no approved tool suggestions exist, exit without changes.

---
description: Build the oldest approved SEO guide suggestion as a PR (guides only)

Call the seo-manager MCP tool `get_instructions` with workflow `build-guide` and follow the returned markdown exactly (it is the current playbook; also read `.dispatchseo/conventions.md` for this repo's site facts). Guides ONLY - type="guide". If no approved guide suggestions exist, exit without changes.

Call the seo-manager MCP tool `get_instructions` with workflow `research` and follow the returned markdown exactly (it is the current playbook; also read `.dispatchseo/conventions.md` for this repo's site facts). Topic scope (optional): $ARGUMENTS

---

---

name: SEO auto-merge

# Hands-off publish gate for the SEO pipeline. Runs when checks report (plus
# an hourly backstop) and merges an open PR labeled `seo` ONLY when both are
# true:
#   1. It is auto-mergeable content:
#      - GUIDE PR: every changed file lives under src/content/blog/,
#        src/components/blog/ (guides ship bespoke visual components), or
#        public/blog/covers/ (the playbook's mandatory cover image).
#      - TOOL PR (label `seo-tool`): carries the `tool-validated` label,
#        meaning seo-tool-validate built the PR, exercised the LIVE page in a
#        real browser, and proved the widget works.
#      Anything else (structural changes, unvalidated tools) waits for the owner.
#   2. Every automated check has PASSED - pnpm build, Vercel preview,
#      CodeRabbit, tool validation. Any pending check -> retry on the next
#      check event. Any failing check -> leave the PR for the owner. Zero checks ->
#      do NOT merge (no gate = not safe).
# This is the "green-checks gate" chosen deliberately over immediate merge, so
# nothing reaches the live, indexed site without passing every automated gate.

on:
  # Event-driven: sweep the moment any check reports, so a green guide PR
  # merges within a minute of its last check instead of waiting for the hour.
  # Both triggers are needed - CodeRabbit reports via the Checks API
  # (check_suite) while Vercel reports via commit statuses (status), and
  # Vercel's deploy is usually the check that finishes last.
  check_suite:
    types: [completed]
  status:
  # Hourly backstop for anything the events miss (e.g. a check that never
  # reports, or GitHub dropping an event).
  schedule:
    - cron: "17 * * * *" # offset off the top of the hour
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: seo-auto-merge
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  merge:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      actions: write # re-run crashed tool validations (self-healing step below)
      checks: read # statusCheckRollup reads check runs (CodeRabbit et al.)
      statuses: read # ...and commit statuses (Vercel)
    steps:
      # Publish-mode gate: the DispatchSEO dashboard controls whether merging
      # is hands-off via the auto_merge automation flag (off in semi mode,
      # on in auto, per-toggle in custom). When it's off the merge step is
      # skipped and green PRs wait on the dashboard's ready-to-ship cards;
      # validation self-healing below still runs so tool PRs keep getting
      # their verdicts either way. A 404 means the backend predates the
      # endpoint - behave as before (merge). Anything else is a real failure
      # and must be loud, not a silent skip.
      - name: Look up the project's automation flags
        id: mode
        env:
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/mode.json -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 30 \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
            {{BACKEND_URL}}/api/project-mode)
          merge=on
          if [ "$code" = "200" ]; then
            if [ "$(jq -r '.automations.auto_merge' /tmp/mode.json)" = "false" ]; then merge=off; fi
            echo "Publish mode: $(jq -r '.mode' /tmp/mode.json) (auto_merge: $merge)"
          elif [ "$code" != "404" ]; then
            echo "::error::project-mode endpoint returned HTTP $code - check the DispatchSEO backend and SEO_MCP_API_KEY"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "merge=$merge" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Merge green SEO PRs
        if: steps.mode.outputs.merge != 'off'
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          prs=$(gh pr list --repo "$REPO" --label seo --state open --json number --jq '.[].number')
          if [ -z "$prs" ]; then
            echo "No open SEO PRs. Nothing to do."
            exit 0
          fi

          for n in $prs; do
            echo "::group::PR #$n"

            # 1. Eligibility gate, by PR shape:
            #    - Tool PR (seo-tool label): must carry tool-validated - the
            #      live smoke-test's pass verdict. Tools legitimately touch
            #      registries/components, so no path check applies.
            #    - Guide PR: every changed file must live in the blog content
            #      dir or the blog components dir (the builder's mandatory
            #      VISUALS step); anything else forces manual review.
            labels=$(gh pr view "$n" --repo "$REPO" --json labels --jq '[.labels[].name] | join(" ")')
            if [[ " $labels " == *" seo-tool "* ]]; then
              if [[ " $labels " != *" tool-validated "* ]]; then
                echo "PR #$n is a tool PR without a tool-validated verdict yet - waiting for validation."
                echo "::endgroup::"
                continue
              fi
              echo "PR #$n is a validated tool PR - checking the status rollup."
            else
              outside=$(gh pr view "$n" --repo "$REPO" --json files \
                --jq '[.files[].path | select((startswith("src/content/blog/") or startswith("src/components/blog/") or startswith("public/blog/covers/")) | not)] | length')
              if [ "$outside" != "0" ]; then
                echo "PR #$n touches $outside file(s) outside the blog dirs (structural change) - leaving for manual review."
                echo "::endgroup::"
                continue
              fi
            fi

            # 2. Checks gate: classify the whole status rollup into one verdict.
            #    StatusContext -> .state ; CheckRun -> .status/.conclusion.
            verdict=$(gh pr view "$n" --repo "$REPO" --json statusCheckRollup --jq '
              [ .statusCheckRollup[] |
                if .__typename == "CheckRun" then
                  ( if .status != "COMPLETED" then "PENDING"
                    elif ((.conclusion // "") | ascii_upcase) as $c
                         | ($c == "SUCCESS" or $c == "NEUTRAL" or $c == "SKIPPED") then "SUCCESS"
                    else "FAIL" end )
                else
                  ( ((.state // "") | ascii_upcase) as $s |
                    if $s == "SUCCESS" then "SUCCESS"
                    elif ($s == "PENDING" or $s == "EXPECTED") then "PENDING"
                    else "FAIL" end )
                end
              ] as $s |
              if ($s | length) == 0 then "NOCHECKS"
              elif ($s | index("FAIL")) then "FAIL"
              elif ($s | index("PENDING")) then "PENDING"
              else "SUCCESS" end')

            case "$verdict" in
              SUCCESS)
                echo "PR #$n has all checks green - merging."
                # Grab the guide slugs BEFORE merging - the merge deletes the
                # branch. Tool PRs yield an empty list; the IndexNow run then
                # skips URL-polling and pings straight from the sitemap.
                slugs=$(gh pr view "$n" --repo "$REPO" --json files \
                  --jq '[.files[].path | select(test("^src/content/blog/.+\\.mdx$")) | sub("^src/content/blog/"; "") | sub("\\.mdx$"; "")] | join(" ")')
                gh pr merge "$n" --repo "$REPO" --squash --delete-branch
                # This squash push is made with GITHUB_TOKEN, and GITHUB_TOKEN
                # pushes never trigger push-event workflows - so the IndexNow
                # workflow would silently skip every auto-merged guide.
                # Dispatch it explicitly (workflow_dispatch is the documented
                # exception to that rule).
                gh workflow run indexnow.yml --repo "$REPO" -f slugs="$slugs" \
                  || echo "IndexNow dispatch failed - the sitemap still covers the URL on the next crawl."
                ;;
              PENDING)
                echo "PR #$n has checks still running - will retry next run."
                ;;
              FAIL)
                echo "PR #$n has a failing check - leaving for the owner."
                ;;
              NOCHECKS)
                echo "PR #$n has no checks reported yet - not merging (no gate)."
                ;;
            esac
            echo "::endgroup::"
          done

      # Self-healing for the tool pipeline. Two ways a tool PR ends up with
      # no verdict label and would otherwise strand silently:
      # - The bot-PR approval gate (GitHub change, 2026-06-11): the builder
      #   opens PRs with the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN, so their pull_request
      #   validation runs are created parked in "action_required" and never
      #   execute. No repo setting disables that, and the approve API rejects
      #   same-repo PRs - but workflow_dispatch events created with
      #   GITHUB_TOKEN always run (the documented exception).
      # - A validator crash (max-turns, infra flake) that ended a run without
      #   applying any verdict label.
      # So each pass: for every verdict-less open tool PR with no validation
      # currently queued or running, dispatch a fresh validation on the PR's
      # branch (the dispatched run resolves and gates the PR itself).
      # Bounded: after 3 executed validations for the same head SHA with no
      # verdict, leave the PR for the owner instead of burning usage all day.
      - name: Dispatch stalled tool validations
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          prs=$(gh pr list --repo "$REPO" --label seo-tool --state open \
            --json number,headRefName,headRefOid,labels \
            --jq '.[] | select([.labels[].name] | (index("tool-validated") or index("tool-validation-failed")) | not) | "\(.number) \(.headRefOid) \(.headRefName)"')
          if [ -z "$prs" ]; then
            echo "No verdict-less tool PRs. Nothing to do."
            exit 0
          fi

          while read -r n sha branch; do
            echo "::group::PR #$n"
            summary=$(gh api "repos/$REPO/actions/workflows/seo-tool-validate.yml/runs?head_sha=$sha&per_page=30" \
              --jq '{active: ([.workflow_runs[] | select(.status != "completed")] | length), executed: ([.workflow_runs[] | select(.status == "completed" and .conclusion != "action_required")] | length), parked: ([.workflow_runs[] | select(.status == "completed" and .conclusion == "action_required")] | length)}')
            active=$(echo "$summary" | jq -r .active)
            executed=$(echo "$summary" | jq -r .executed)
            parked=$(echo "$summary" | jq -r .parked)
            echo "Validation runs for $sha: active=$active executed=$executed parked=$parked"
            if [ "$active" != "0" ]; then
              echo "A validation is already queued or running - waiting for its verdict."
            elif [ "$executed" -ge 3 ]; then
              echo "$executed validations executed with no verdict - leaving PR #$n for the owner."
            else
              echo "Dispatching a fresh validation on $branch (approval-gated or crashed run)."
              gh workflow run seo-tool-validate.yml --repo "$REPO" --ref "$branch" -f pr_number="$n" \
                || echo "::warning::Dispatch failed - if this branch predates the dispatch-mode validator, merge or validate PR #$n manually."
            fi
            echo "::endgroup::"
          done <<< "$prs"

name: SEO install canary

# Install-time proof that the whole PR machinery works. setup.sh dispatches
# this once at the end of an install: it creates a throwaway branch, opens a
# real PR from inside a workflow (the exact thing GitHub's default settings
# block on new repos), then closes it and deletes the branch - nothing is
# ever merged, no deploy is triggered. If any switch is wrong, the install
# fails HERE, in front of the owner, instead of on day 3 at 05:00.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  canary:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Open and close a throwaway PR from a workflow
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          branch="dispatchseo-canary-${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git checkout -b "$branch"
          mkdir -p .dispatchseo
          echo "run ${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" > .dispatchseo/canary
          git add .dispatchseo/canary
          git commit -m "chore: DispatchSEO install canary (auto-closed, never merged)"
          git push origin "$branch"
          pr=$(gh pr create --title "DispatchSEO install canary (auto-closes)" \
            --body "Throwaway PR proving GitHub Actions may open pull requests on this repo. Closed automatically by the same run - never merge this." \
            --base "${GITHUB_REF_NAME}" --head "$branch")
          echo "Canary PR created: $pr"
          gh pr close "$pr" --delete-branch
          echo "Canary PR closed and branch deleted - PR machinery verified."

      - name: Clean up the canary branch if the run failed
        if: failure()
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          git push origin --delete "dispatchseo-canary-${GITHUB_RUN_ID}" 2>/dev/null || true

      - name: Report result to the dashboard
        if: always()
        env:
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
          OUTCOME: ${{ job.status }}
        run: |
          if [ "$OUTCOME" = "success" ]; then
            curl -sG --max-time 30 -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
              --data-urlencode "job=seo-canary" --data-urlencode "ok=1" \
              "{{BACKEND_URL}}/api/cron/deploy-check" || true
          else
            curl -sG --max-time 30 -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
              --data-urlencode "job=seo-canary" \
              --data-urlencode "fail=install canary could not open a PR - enable 'Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests' (repo Settings -> Actions -> General) and re-run setup" \
              "{{BACKEND_URL}}/api/cron/deploy-check" || true
          fi

name: SEO daily builder

# Every morning, build the oldest approved suggestion (guide or tool) into a PR,
# fully autonomously, through the centrally-served DispatchSEO pipeline (get_instructions -> template -> live
# SERP gate -> humanizer -> PR). If nothing is approved, it exits cleanly. Skips
# if an SEO PR is already open, so there is never more than one at a time - the
# steady state is build -> auto-merge -> gone -> repeat, and if a PR gets stuck
# (failed check, or a tool PR awaiting you) the builder pauses instead of piling
# more on top. Publishing is handled by seo-auto-merge.yml: guide PRs whose
# checks all pass are merged hands-off; tool PRs are left for you to merge.

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 5 * * *" # 05:00 UTC daily
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: seo-build
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 45
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Skip if an SEO PR is already open or guide builds are toggled off
        id: guard
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          open=$(gh pr list --repo "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY" --label seo --state open --json number --jq 'length')
          if [ "$open" != "0" ]; then
            echo "An SEO PR is already open - skipping this run so only one is ever open at a time."
          fi
          # Automation-flag gate: the DispatchSEO dashboard can pause guide
          # builds (auto_build_guides off). 404 = backend without the endpoint
          # yet -> build as before; other failures are loud.
          if [ "$open" = "0" ]; then
            code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/mode.json -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 30 \
              -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
              {{BACKEND_URL}}/api/project-mode)
            if [ "$code" = "200" ] && [ "$(jq -r '.automations.auto_build_guides' /tmp/mode.json)" = "false" ]; then
              echo "Guide builds are toggled off on the dashboard - skipping."
              open=paused
            elif [ "$code" != "200" ] && [ "$code" != "404" ]; then
              echo "::error::project-mode endpoint returned HTTP $code"; exit 1
            fi
          fi
          echo "open=$open" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      # Fail fast, in plain language, when the Claude token secret is broken.
      # Without this the claude-code-action dies minutes in with a cryptic
      # "Environment variable validation failed" (empty secret) or a 401
      # (wrapped/wrong token) - the 2026-07-17 dogfood install burned four
      # runs on exactly that. setup.sh verifies the token before storing it,
      # so this preflight should only ever fire on hand-set secrets.
      - name: Preflight - Claude token secret usable
        if: steps.guard.outputs.open == '0'
        env:
          CLAUDE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$CLAUDE_TOKEN" ]; then
            echo "::error::The CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN secret is missing or empty on this repo. Rerun the setup command from your DispatchSEO dashboard (it mints and VERIFIES a fresh token), or set one by hand: run 'claude setup-token', then pipe the token into 'gh secret set CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN' without any whitespace."
            exit 1
          fi
          case "$CLAUDE_TOKEN" in
            sk-ant-oat*) echo "Claude token present." ;;
            *)
              echo "::error::CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN does not look like a Claude Code OAuth token (expected it to start with sk-ant-oat). It was probably line-wrapped or the wrong text was pasted when it was saved. Rerun the setup command from your DispatchSEO dashboard to mint and verify a replacement."
              exit 1 ;;
          esac

      # Fail loudly if the seo-manager MCP is unreachable. Without this, a
      # stale URL in mcp-ci.json makes Claude see an empty toolset and "exit
      # cleanly" - a green run that built nothing (2026-07-14 domain-move bug).
      - name: Preflight - seo-manager MCP reachable
        if: steps.guard.outputs.open == '0'
        env:
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          url=$(node -e "console.log(require('./.github/mcp-ci.json').mcpServers['seo-manager'].url)")
          code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 30 -X POST "$url" \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
            -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":0,"method":"tools/list"}')
          if [ "$code" != "200" ]; then
            echo "::error::seo-manager MCP at $url returned HTTP $code - fix the URL in .github/mcp-ci.json or the SEO_MCP_API_KEY secret"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "seo-manager MCP OK ($url)"

      - name: Set up pnpm
        # INSTALL-ADAPT: if this repo's package.json has a packageManager
        # field ("pnpm@x.y.z"), DELETE the version input below - action-setup
        # reads the field itself, and a version input alongside packageManager
        # hard-errors on any mismatch (plain string compare, not semver). If
        # there is no packageManager field, keep a version here matching the
        # major that wrote pnpm-lock.yaml. pnpm 11+ also requires Node 22+.
        if: steps.guard.outputs.open == '0'
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 11

      - name: Set up Node
        if: steps.guard.outputs.open == '0'
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: pnpm

      - name: Install deps (so the pipeline's `pnpm build` verify step works)
        if: steps.guard.outputs.open == '0'
        run: |
          pnpm install --frozen-lockfile || {
            s=$?
            echo "::error::pnpm install failed (exit $s). Frequent causes: (1) ERR_PNPM_IGNORED_BUILDS - pnpm 11 hard-fails dependencies with unanswered build scripts (strictDepBuilds is on by default): answer each one in pnpm-workspace.yaml (allowBuilds: dep: true/false, plus an onlyBuiltDependencies list for older pnpm) and commit. (2) pnpm/lockfile version mismatch - align the workflow's pnpm with the version that wrote pnpm-lock.yaml (package.json packageManager field). Reproduce locally with a fresh 'pnpm install --frozen-lockfile'."
            exit $s
          }

      - name: Run the SEO builder
        if: steps.guard.outputs.open == '0'
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          # Pass the standard workflow token as an input so the action uses it
          # directly instead of exchanging OIDC for a Claude GitHub App token
          # (which would require installing the app on the repo).
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          prompt: "FIRST call the seo-manager MCP tool get_instructions with workflow build-guide, then follow the returned markdown exactly - it is the current playbook and overrides any cached knowledge of this pipeline. Also read .dispatchseo/conventions.md for this repo's site facts. In brief: take the oldest approved guide suggestion, mark it in_progress, build the guide MDX through the full pipeline (template, thin-content gate, draft, mandatory visuals, humanizer), run pnpm build to verify, open a PR labeled seo via gh, then update_suggestion to done with the PR url and log_page, and state the instructions version in the run report. Never build tool suggestions - those belong to the seo-build-tool workflow. If get_instructions is unavailable, fail loudly and exit without changes; if there are no approved guide suggestions, exit cleanly without any changes."
          claude_args: |
            --mcp-config ./.github/mcp-ci.json
            --permission-mode bypassPermissions
            --max-turns 150
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
          DATAFORSEO_LOGIN: ${{ secrets.DATAFORSEO_LOGIN }}
          DATAFORSEO_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.DATAFORSEO_PASSWORD }}
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          MCP_TIMEOUT: "120000"
          # Placeholder public env so `next build` never trips on missing vars -
          # static blog pages do not call Supabase, and the clients are lazy.
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: "https://placeholder.supabase.co"
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: "placeholder-anon-key"
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL: "https://{{DOMAIN}}"

      # The action hides Claude's transcript (these logs can be public), which
      # also hides the API error when a run dies on its first call - a dead
      # token shows only "is_error:true" with no way to tell a revoked token
      # from a usage limit. On failure, surface JUST the final result/error
      # message from the execution log the action saves; GitHub's secret
      # masking still applies to whatever prints.
      - name: Surface Claude's error message on failure
        if: failure() && steps.guard.outputs.open == '0'
        run: |
          f="$RUNNER_TEMP/claude-execution-output.json"
          [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "No execution log at $f."; exit 0; }
          jq -r 'if type=="array" then .[] else . end
                 | select(.type? == "result")
                 | "Claude result: is_error=\(.is_error) subtype=\(.subtype) message=\(.result // .error // "none")"' \
            "$f" 2>/dev/null | tail -3 || echo "Could not parse $f"

name: SEO geo scan

# Every Wednesday, measure AI visibility: the agent asks the questions
# {{SITE_NAME}}'s customers would ask an AI assistant (built from the tracked
# keywords), answers them with real web search on the owner's own Claude
# subscription, and records which answers cite {{DOMAIN}} via the seo-manager
# MCP. Google's AI Overview side needs no agent - the daily rank cron already
# snapshots it. No PR - results land on the dashboard's AI-visibility section.

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 6 * * 3" # Wednesdays 06:00 UTC
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: seo-geo-scan
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  geo-scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Fail fast, in plain language, when the Claude token secret is broken.
      - name: Preflight - Claude token secret usable
        env:
          CLAUDE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$CLAUDE_TOKEN" ]; then
            echo "::error::The CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN secret is missing or empty on this repo. Rerun the setup command from your DispatchSEO dashboard (it mints and VERIFIES a fresh token), or set one by hand: run 'claude setup-token', then pipe the token into 'gh secret set CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN' without any whitespace."
            exit 1
          fi
          case "$CLAUDE_TOKEN" in
            sk-ant-oat*) echo "Claude token present." ;;
            *)
              echo "::error::CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN does not look like a Claude Code OAuth token (expected it to start with sk-ant-oat). It was probably line-wrapped or the wrong text was pasted when it was saved. Rerun the setup command from your DispatchSEO dashboard to mint and verify a replacement."
              exit 1 ;;
          esac

      # Fail loudly if the seo-manager MCP is unreachable - without this a
      # stale URL makes Claude see an empty toolset and "exit cleanly".
      - name: Preflight - seo-manager MCP reachable
        env:
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          url=$(node -e "console.log(require('./.github/mcp-ci.json').mcpServers['seo-manager'].url)")
          code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 30 -X POST "$url" \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
            -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":0,"method":"tools/list"}')
          if [ "$code" != "200" ]; then
            echo "::error::seo-manager MCP at $url returned HTTP $code - fix the URL in .github/mcp-ci.json or the SEO_MCP_API_KEY secret"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "seo-manager MCP OK ($url)"

      - name: Run the geo scan
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          show_full_output: true
          prompt: "FIRST call the seo-manager MCP tool get_instructions with workflow geo-scan, then follow the returned markdown exactly - it is the current playbook and overrides any cached knowledge of this pipeline. In brief: build ~15 customer questions from the tracked keywords and conventions, answer each with REAL web search (never from memory), judge whether the site is among the cited sources, record every result via record_ai_citations with verbatim answer excerpts, then read get_ai_visibility and report the citation counts and gap domains. If get_instructions is unavailable, fail loudly and exit without changes."
          claude_args: |
            --mcp-config ./.github/mcp-ci.json
            --permission-mode bypassPermissions
            --max-turns 120
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
          MCP_TIMEOUT: "120000"

name: SEO health check

# Daily pre-flight, 30 minutes before the 05:00 builder: proves the Claude
# token still works with one minimal model call, and checks whether the
# installed pipeline is stale. Both outcomes phone home to the DispatchSEO
# dashboard (banner + email on failure), so a dead token is a red alert at
# 04:30 - not a mystery failed build at 05:00. Cost: one tiny Claude call per
# day on the owner's own subscription.

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "30 4 * * *" # 04:30 UTC daily, before the 05:00 builder
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  health:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # One minimal model call. If the token is dead/revoked/limited, this
      # step fails and the failure handler below reports the real reason.
      - name: Claude token liveness
        id: liveness
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          prompt: "Reply with exactly: ok"
          claude_args: |
            --max-turns 1

      - name: Report token OK
        if: success()
        env:
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          curl -sG --max-time 30 -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
            --data-urlencode "job=seo-token-check" --data-urlencode "ok=1" \
            "{{BACKEND_URL}}/api/cron/deploy-check" || true

      # Surface the real API error (revoked? usage limit?) and phone it home.
      # Full transcripts stay hidden - only the final result line is read.
      - name: Report token failure with the reason
        if: failure()
        env:
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          f="$RUNNER_TEMP/claude-execution-output.json"
          reason="token check failed (no error detail captured)"
          if [ -f "$f" ]; then
            msg=$(jq -r 'if type=="array" then .[] else . end
                         | select(.type? == "result")
                         | (.result // .error // empty)' "$f" 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
            [ -n "$msg" ] && reason="Claude token check failed: $msg"
          fi
          echo "::error::$reason - mint a fresh token (claude setup-token) and re-run the setup command from your DispatchSEO dashboard."
          curl -sG --max-time 30 -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
            --data-urlencode "job=seo-token-check" \
            --data-urlencode "fail=$reason - re-run the setup command to mint a fresh token" \
            "{{BACKEND_URL}}/api/cron/deploy-check" || true

      # Pipeline staleness: compare this repo's installed pack version stamp
      # against the backend's current one. Stale is not broken - the report
      # tells the owner an update is waiting; re-running the setup command
      # applies it. Repos installed before version stamps skip silently.
      - name: Pipeline update check
        if: always()
        env:
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          [ -f .dispatchseo/pipeline-version ] || { echo "No version stamp (pre-stamp install) - skipping."; exit 0; }
          installed=$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .dispatchseo/pipeline-version)
          current=$(curl -s --max-time 30 -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
            "{{BACKEND_URL}}/api/project-mode" | jq -r '.pipeline_version // empty')
          if [ -z "$current" ]; then echo "Backend has no pipeline_version yet - skipping."; exit 0; fi
          if [ "$installed" = "$current" ]; then
            echo "Pipeline up to date ($installed)."
            curl -sG --max-time 30 -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
              --data-urlencode "job=seo-pipeline-version" --data-urlencode "ok=1" \
              "{{BACKEND_URL}}/api/cron/deploy-check" || true
          else
            echo "::warning::Pipeline update available (installed $installed, current $current). Re-run the setup command from your DispatchSEO dashboard to apply it."
            curl -sG --max-time 30 -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
              --data-urlencode "job=seo-pipeline-version" \
              --data-urlencode "fail=pipeline update available (installed $installed, current $current) - re-run the setup command to apply" \
              "{{BACKEND_URL}}/api/cron/deploy-check" || true
          fi

name: SEO tool validation

# The functional reviewer for autonomously-built tool PRs. A tool PR may only
# auto-merge if this workflow has exercised the LIVE page (real production
# build, real browser) and proven the widget works. On pass it labels the PR
# `tool-validated`; the auto-merge gate requires that label for any PR that
# carries `seo-tool`. On fail it labels `tool-validation-failed` and comments
# the concrete failures so the next builder run (or the owner) can fix them.
#
# Scope: runs ONLY on same-repo PRs labeled seo-tool (the tool builder's PRs).
# Guide PRs never get the seo-tool label and are never gated by this.
#
# Two triggers for the same validation:
# - pull_request: the normal path. BUT tool PRs are opened by the builder
#   with the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN, and since GitHub's 2026-06-11 change
#   those runs are created parked in "action_required" (awaiting manual
#   approval, with no repo-level opt-out) - left alone the PR stalls forever.
# - workflow_dispatch (pr_number input): the escape hatch. Dispatch events
#   created with GITHUB_TOKEN always execute (documented exception), so
#   seo-auto-merge's self-heal dispatches this workflow for any verdict-less
#   tool PR. Both paths converge on the resolve step below, which re-verifies
#   the same-repo + seo-tool gates from live PR state.

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, labeled]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      pr_number:
        description: "Open seo-tool PR number to validate"
        required: true

concurrency:
  group: seo-tool-validate-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || inputs.pr_number }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  validate:
    # pull_request mode: same-repo PRs only (fork PRs get no secrets anyway)
    # + must be a tool PR. Dispatch mode passes here and is gated by the
    # resolve step instead (its event payload has no PR fields to check).
    if: >
      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||
      (github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository &&
       contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'seo-tool'))
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    # SECURITY: this job builds and RUNS the PR's (LLM-authored) code, so it
    # must never hold a token that can write to the repo. Labels/comments only;
    # the squash-merge lives in the separate `merge` job below, which never
    # checks out or executes PR code.
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
      issues: write # gh label create
    steps:
      # Authoritative gate for BOTH trigger modes: read the PR's live state
      # and refuse anything that is not an open, same-repo, seo-tool PR. Also
      # resolves the head SHA the job checks out, so a dispatched run
      # validates exactly the PR head (not the default branch the dispatch
      # technically runs against).
      - name: Resolve and gate the PR
        id: pr
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number || inputs.pr_number }}
        run: |
          case "$PR_NUMBER" in
            ''|*[!0-9]*) echo "::error::pr_number must be a plain PR number."; exit 1 ;;
          esac
          info=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --repo "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY" \
            --json state,isCrossRepository,labels,headRefOid \
            --jq '{state, cross: .isCrossRepository, labels: [.labels[].name], sha: .headRefOid}')
          echo "$info"
          if [ "$(echo "$info" | jq -r .state)" != "OPEN" ] \
             || [ "$(echo "$info" | jq -r .cross)" != "false" ] \
             || ! echo "$info" | jq -e '.labels | index("seo-tool")' >/dev/null; then
            echo "::error::PR #$PR_NUMBER is not an open same-repo seo-tool PR - refusing to validate."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "sha=$(echo "$info" | jq -r .sha)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          # The resolved PR head, so both trigger modes exercise the same code.
          ref: ${{ steps.pr.outputs.sha }}
          # Don't leave a git credential on disk for the PR's build scripts to
          # read - this job's steps talk to GitHub only via the env tokens on
          # the specific steps that need them.
          persist-credentials: false

      # Fail fast, in plain language, when the Claude token secret is broken.
      # Without this the claude-code-action dies minutes in with a cryptic
      # "Environment variable validation failed" (empty secret) or a 401
      # (wrapped/wrong token) - the 2026-07-17 dogfood install burned four
      # runs on exactly that. setup.sh verifies the token before storing it,
      # so this preflight should only ever fire on hand-set secrets.
      - name: Preflight - Claude token secret usable
        env:
          CLAUDE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$CLAUDE_TOKEN" ]; then
            echo "::error::The CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN secret is missing or empty on this repo. Rerun the setup command from your DispatchSEO dashboard (it mints and VERIFIES a fresh token), or set one by hand: run 'claude setup-token', then pipe the token into 'gh secret set CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN' without any whitespace."
            exit 1
          fi
          case "$CLAUDE_TOKEN" in
            sk-ant-oat*) echo "Claude token present." ;;
            *)
              echo "::error::CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN does not look like a Claude Code OAuth token (expected it to start with sk-ant-oat). It was probably line-wrapped or the wrong text was pasted when it was saved. Rerun the setup command from your DispatchSEO dashboard to mint and verify a replacement."
              exit 1 ;;
          esac

      - name: Set up pnpm
        # INSTALL-ADAPT: if this repo's package.json has a packageManager
        # field ("pnpm@x.y.z"), DELETE the version input below - action-setup
        # reads the field itself, and a version input alongside packageManager
        # hard-errors on any mismatch (plain string compare, not semver). If
        # there is no packageManager field, keep a version here matching the
        # major that wrote pnpm-lock.yaml. pnpm 11+ also requires Node 22+.
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 11

      - name: Set up Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: pnpm

      - name: Install deps
        run: |
          pnpm install --frozen-lockfile || {
            s=$?
            echo "::error::pnpm install failed (exit $s). Frequent causes: (1) ERR_PNPM_IGNORED_BUILDS - pnpm 11 hard-fails dependencies with unanswered build scripts (strictDepBuilds is on by default): answer each one in pnpm-workspace.yaml (allowBuilds: dep: true/false, plus an onlyBuiltDependencies list for older pnpm) and commit. (2) pnpm/lockfile version mismatch - align the workflow's pnpm with the version that wrote pnpm-lock.yaml (package.json packageManager field). Reproduce locally with a fresh 'pnpm install --frozen-lockfile'."
            exit $s
          }

      - name: Production build
        run: pnpm build
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: "https://placeholder.supabase.co"
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: "placeholder-anon-key"
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL: "http://localhost:3000"

      - name: Start server and wait for it
        run: |
          nohup pnpm start >/tmp/next-server.log 2>&1 &
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000/ || true)
            [ "$code" = "200" ] && echo "server up" && exit 0
            sleep 2
          done
          echo "server failed to start"; cat /tmp/next-server.log; exit 1
        env:
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: "https://placeholder.supabase.co"
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: "placeholder-anon-key"
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL: "http://localhost:3000"

      - name: Install Playwright browser for the MCP
        run: npx -y playwright install --with-deps chromium

      - name: Validate the tool live
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          # Tool PRs are opened and pushed by the builder workflow, so the
          # triggering actor is github-actions[bot]; without this the action
          # refuses to start ("non-human actor") and the PR never gets a
          # verdict. Same-repo + seo-tool label gates above still apply.
          allowed_bots: "github-actions"
          # Full transcript in the step log - post-hoc debuggability.
          show_full_output: true
          prompt: "You are the functional validator for an autonomously-built {{SITE_NAME}} tool PR. 1) Find the tool under review: run `git diff origin/main...HEAD --name-only` and read the diff of the tools registry (its path is listed in .dispatchseo/conventions.md) to get the NEW tool's slug (the entry added in this PR). 2) Using the playwright MCP, open http://localhost:3000/tools/<slug> and validate: the page returns 200 and renders the landing funnel (hero h1, the interactive widget, FAQ section); every primary widget interaction works (fill each input with realistic values, click every action button, verify output actually updates, test the copy action); edge cases do not break it (empty input, nonsense input - the widget must never throw); zero console errors during the whole session (check browser console messages). 3) Verdict: if EVERYTHING passes, run `gh pr edit <pr-number> --add-label tool-validated --remove-label tool-validation-failed || gh pr edit <pr-number> --add-label tool-validated` and post a short PR comment via `gh pr comment` summarizing what you exercised. If ANYTHING fails, run `gh pr edit <pr-number> --add-label tool-validation-failed --remove-label tool-validated || gh pr edit <pr-number> --add-label tool-validation-failed` (removing tool-validated matters: a stale pass from an earlier commit must not authorize a merge) and comment the concrete failures (what you did, what happened, console output). Create missing labels with `gh label create` if needed. The PR number is in the PR_NUMBER environment variable. Never modify code in this run - you are a reviewer only."
          claude_args: |
            --mcp-config ./.github/mcp-validate.json
            --permission-mode bypassPermissions
            --max-turns 140
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number || inputs.pr_number }}

  # The hands-off publish for tools: a PASS verdict from the live validation
  # above IS the merge authorization (the tool was built in prod mode and
  # exercised in a real browser - a stronger gate than any preview check).
  # FAIL or no verdict leaves the PR open for the owner.
  #
  # SECURITY: this is a separate job on a fresh runner precisely so the
  # contents:write token never coexists with the PR's executed code (the
  # validate job above builds and runs the branch). No checkout here - it only
  # reads labels and merges via gh.
  merge:
    needs: validate
    # Dispatch mode passes here by design: the validate job's resolve step
    # already enforced the same-repo + seo-tool gates, and this job re-reads
    # the labels via gh before acting anyway.
    if: >
      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||
      (github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository &&
       contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'seo-tool'))
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    permissions:
      contents: write # squash-merge + branch delete
      pull-requests: write
    steps:
      - name: Merge on PASS verdict
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number || inputs.pr_number }}
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          # Publish-mode gate: same rule as seo-auto-merge - when the owner
          # turned auto_merge off (semi mode or a custom toggle), a PASS still
          # labels the PR but the merge waits for the dashboard's Merge click.
          code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/mode.json -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 30 \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
            {{BACKEND_URL}}/api/project-mode)
          if [ "$code" = "200" ] && [ "$(jq -r '.automations.auto_merge' /tmp/mode.json)" = "false" ]; then
            echo "auto_merge is off on the dashboard - leaving the validated PR for the owner."
            exit 0
          elif [ "$code" != "200" ] && [ "$code" != "404" ]; then
            echo "::error::project-mode endpoint returned HTTP $code"; exit 1
          fi

          labels=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --repo "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY" --json labels -q '.labels[].name')
          # Both conditions: tool-validated present AND no failure flag. A stale
          # tool-validated from an earlier commit must never merge a newer,
          # failing state of the branch.
          if echo "$labels" | grep -qx "tool-validated" && ! echo "$labels" | grep -qx "tool-validation-failed"; then
            echo "Validated - squash-merging PR #$PR_NUMBER."
            gh pr merge "$PR_NUMBER" --repo "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY" --squash --delete-branch || {
              gh pr comment "$PR_NUMBER" --repo "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY" --body "Validation passed but auto-merge failed (likely a conflict with main). Manual merge needed."
              exit 1
            }
            # The squash push above is made with GITHUB_TOKEN, which never
            # triggers push-event workflows - dispatch IndexNow explicitly so
            # the new tool URL gets pinged (no blog slugs; the ping reads the
            # live sitemap).
            gh workflow run indexnow.yml --repo "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY" \
              || echo "IndexNow dispatch failed - the sitemap still covers the URL on the next crawl."
          else
            echo "No tool-validated label - leaving the PR open for review."
          fi

name: SEO tool builder

# Builds the oldest APPROVED tool suggestion into a PR through the seo-manager
# skill's /seo-build-tool pipeline (claude-md-generator template -> live SERP
# gate -> theme discovery -> execution plan + value bar -> build -> humanizer
# -> PR labeled seo + seo-tool).
#
# Tools are approve-idea-first: nothing builds until the user greenlights the
# concept on the dashboard. Three triggers:
#   1. repository_dispatch "seo-tool-approved" - the dashboard fires this the
#      moment a tool suggestion is approved, so the build starts immediately.
#   2. Weekly sweep (Wednesday 05:30 UTC) - fallback that catches anything the
#      dispatch missed (backend hiccup, approval made while Actions was down).
#   3. workflow_dispatch - manual runs.
# If the queue has no approved tools, the run exits cleanly.
#
# Merging: tool PRs are NOT merged by this workflow. The seo-tool-validate
# workflow smoke-tests the built tool live and labels it tool-validated; the
# auto-merge gate only merges tool PRs carrying that label.

on:
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [seo-tool-approved]
  schedule:
    - cron: "30 5 * * 3" # Wednesdays 05:30 UTC - researcher fills the queue Monday
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: seo-tool-build
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 45
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Skip if a tool PR is already open or tool builds are toggled off
        id: guard
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          open=$(gh pr list --repo "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY" --label seo-tool --state open --json number --jq 'length')
          if [ "$open" != "0" ]; then
            echo "A tool PR is already open - skipping. The suggestion stays approved; the weekly sweep will pick it up once the open PR lands."
          fi
          # Automation-flag gate: the DispatchSEO dashboard can pause tool
          # builds (auto_build_tools off). 404 = backend without the endpoint
          # yet -> build as before; other failures are loud.
          if [ "$open" = "0" ]; then
            code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/mode.json -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 30 \
              -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
              {{BACKEND_URL}}/api/project-mode)
            if [ "$code" = "200" ] && [ "$(jq -r '.automations.auto_build_tools' /tmp/mode.json)" = "false" ]; then
              echo "Tool builds are toggled off on the dashboard - skipping. The suggestion stays approved."
              open=paused
            elif [ "$code" != "200" ] && [ "$code" != "404" ]; then
              echo "::error::project-mode endpoint returned HTTP $code"; exit 1
            fi
          fi
          echo "open=$open" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      # Fail fast, in plain language, when the Claude token secret is broken.
      # Without this the claude-code-action dies minutes in with a cryptic
      # "Environment variable validation failed" (empty secret) or a 401
      # (wrapped/wrong token) - the 2026-07-17 dogfood install burned four
      # runs on exactly that. setup.sh verifies the token before storing it,
      # so this preflight should only ever fire on hand-set secrets.
      - name: Preflight - Claude token secret usable
        if: steps.guard.outputs.open == '0'
        env:
          CLAUDE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$CLAUDE_TOKEN" ]; then
            echo "::error::The CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN secret is missing or empty on this repo. Rerun the setup command from your DispatchSEO dashboard (it mints and VERIFIES a fresh token), or set one by hand: run 'claude setup-token', then pipe the token into 'gh secret set CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN' without any whitespace."
            exit 1
          fi
          case "$CLAUDE_TOKEN" in
            sk-ant-oat*) echo "Claude token present." ;;
            *)
              echo "::error::CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN does not look like a Claude Code OAuth token (expected it to start with sk-ant-oat). It was probably line-wrapped or the wrong text was pasted when it was saved. Rerun the setup command from your DispatchSEO dashboard to mint and verify a replacement."
              exit 1 ;;
          esac

      # Fail loudly if the seo-manager MCP is unreachable. Without this, a
      # stale URL in mcp-ci.json makes Claude see an empty toolset and "exit
      # cleanly" - a green run that built nothing (2026-07-14 domain-move bug).
      - name: Preflight - seo-manager MCP reachable
        if: steps.guard.outputs.open == '0'
        env:
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          url=$(node -e "console.log(require('./.github/mcp-ci.json').mcpServers['seo-manager'].url)")
          code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 30 -X POST "$url" \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
            -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":0,"method":"tools/list"}')
          if [ "$code" != "200" ]; then
            echo "::error::seo-manager MCP at $url returned HTTP $code - fix the URL in .github/mcp-ci.json or the SEO_MCP_API_KEY secret"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "seo-manager MCP OK ($url)"

      - name: Set up pnpm
        # INSTALL-ADAPT: if this repo's package.json has a packageManager
        # field ("pnpm@x.y.z"), DELETE the version input below - action-setup
        # reads the field itself, and a version input alongside packageManager
        # hard-errors on any mismatch (plain string compare, not semver). If
        # there is no packageManager field, keep a version here matching the
        # major that wrote pnpm-lock.yaml. pnpm 11+ also requires Node 22+.
        if: steps.guard.outputs.open == '0'
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 11

      - name: Set up Node
        if: steps.guard.outputs.open == '0'
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: pnpm

      - name: Install deps (so the skill's `pnpm build` verify step works)
        if: steps.guard.outputs.open == '0'
        run: |
          pnpm install --frozen-lockfile || {
            s=$?
            echo "::error::pnpm install failed (exit $s). Frequent causes: (1) ERR_PNPM_IGNORED_BUILDS - pnpm 11 hard-fails dependencies with unanswered build scripts (strictDepBuilds is on by default): answer each one in pnpm-workspace.yaml (allowBuilds: dep: true/false, plus an onlyBuiltDependencies list for older pnpm) and commit. (2) pnpm/lockfile version mismatch - align the workflow's pnpm with the version that wrote pnpm-lock.yaml (package.json packageManager field). Reproduce locally with a fresh 'pnpm install --frozen-lockfile'."
            exit $s
          }

      - name: Run the SEO tool builder
        if: steps.guard.outputs.open == '0'
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          # Standard workflow token as input so the action skips the Claude
          # GitHub App exchange (same pattern as seo-daily.yml).
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          # Full transcript in the step log - post-hoc debuggability.
          show_full_output: true
          prompt: "FIRST call the seo-manager MCP tool get_instructions with workflow build-tool, then follow the returned markdown exactly - it is the current playbook and overrides any cached knowledge of this pipeline. Also read .dispatchseo/conventions.md for this repo's site facts (registry wiring, reference implementation, theme tokens). In brief: take the oldest approved tool suggestion, mark it in_progress, read the reference implementation completely, pass the live SERP gate, run the THEME step, write the mandatory EXECUTION PLAN and hold it against the VALUE BAR - a re-skinned template or canned-output widget is a failure, redesign or set the suggestion back to pending. Then build, humanize all registry copy, run pnpm build to verify plus the funnel composition check, open a PR labeled seo and seo-tool via gh with the execution plan in the body, then update_suggestion to done with the PR url and log_page, and state the instructions version in the run report. Never build guide suggestions - those belong to the daily seo-build workflow. If get_instructions is unavailable, fail loudly and exit without changes; if there are no approved tool suggestions, exit cleanly without any changes."
          claude_args: |
            --mcp-config ./.github/mcp-ci.json
            --permission-mode bypassPermissions
            --max-turns 150
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
          DATAFORSEO_LOGIN: ${{ secrets.DATAFORSEO_LOGIN }}
          DATAFORSEO_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.DATAFORSEO_PASSWORD }}
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          MCP_TIMEOUT: "120000"
          # Placeholder public env so `next build` never trips on missing vars.
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: "https://placeholder.supabase.co"
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: "placeholder-anon-key"
          NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL: "https://{{DOMAIN}}"

name: SEO trend expand

# Stage 2 of the two-stage trend radar: fires when the owner hits Get takes
# on a radar subject (the dashboard sends a seo-trend-expand
# repository_dispatch carrying the topic id + title). The run turns that ONE
# subject into 3-5 validated guide angles, queued as PENDING suggestions
# linked to the topic - the owner then queues or builds the takes they like.
# This is where SERP validation spends money, which is exactly why it only
# runs on subjects the owner picked. The payload comes from the DispatchSEO
# backend only (repository_dispatch needs a write token), so interpolating
# it into the prompt is trusted input here.

on:
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [seo-trend-expand]

concurrency:
  group: seo-trend-expand
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  expand:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # SERP validation makes this a little longer than the scan; past 20
    # minutes it's a hang burning quota.
    timeout-minutes: 20
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Fail fast, in plain language, when the Claude token secret is broken.
      # Without this the claude-code-action dies minutes in with a cryptic
      # "Environment variable validation failed" (empty secret) or a 401
      # (wrapped/wrong token) - the 2026-07-17 dogfood install burned four
      # runs on exactly that. setup.sh verifies the token before storing it,
      # so this preflight should only ever fire on hand-set secrets.
      - name: Preflight - Claude token secret usable
        env:
          CLAUDE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$CLAUDE_TOKEN" ]; then
            echo "::error::The CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN secret is missing or empty on this repo. Rerun the setup command from your DispatchSEO dashboard (it mints and VERIFIES a fresh token), or set one by hand: run 'claude setup-token', then pipe the token into 'gh secret set CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN' without any whitespace."
            exit 1
          fi
          case "$CLAUDE_TOKEN" in
            sk-ant-oat*) echo "Claude token present." ;;
            *)
              echo "::error::CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN does not look like a Claude Code OAuth token (expected it to start with sk-ant-oat). It was probably line-wrapped or the wrong text was pasted when it was saved. Rerun the setup command from your DispatchSEO dashboard to mint and verify a replacement."
              exit 1 ;;
          esac

      # Same fail-loudly preflight as the scan: an unreachable MCP must never
      # produce a green run that expanded nothing.
      - name: Preflight - seo-manager MCP reachable
        env:
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          url=$(node -e "console.log(require('./.github/mcp-ci.json').mcpServers['seo-manager'].url)")
          code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 30 -X POST "$url" \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $SEO_MCP_API_KEY" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -H "Accept: application/json, text/event-stream" \
            -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":0,"method":"tools/list"}')
          if [ "$code" != "200" ]; then
            echo "::error::seo-manager MCP at $url returned HTTP $code - fix the URL in .github/mcp-ci.json or the SEO_MCP_API_KEY secret"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "seo-manager MCP OK ($url)"

      - name: Expand the picked subject into takes
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          show_full_output: true
          prompt: |
            FIRST call the seo-manager MCP tool get_instructions with workflow trend-expand, then follow the returned markdown exactly - it is the current playbook and overrides any cached knowledge of this pipeline. Also read .dispatchseo/conventions.md for what this product is.

            The subject the owner picked: topic_id "${{ github.event.client_payload.topic_id }}", title "${{ github.event.client_payload.topic_title }}".

            In brief: read the topic's evidence via get_trend_topics (fail loudly if the id is missing or dismissed), draft 3-5 distinct takes on the subject, validate each with suggest_keywords + check_serp + product fit, queue the survivors via propose_suggestion with source trend-scan AND this trend_topic_id - all as PENDING, never approved - then mark the topic expanded via update_trend_topic and report the instructions version. Fewer strong takes beat five thin ones; "no viable takes" is an honest, valid outcome. If get_instructions is unavailable, fail loudly and exit without changes.
          claude_args: |
            --mcp-config ./.github/mcp-ci.json
            --permission-mode bypassPermissions
            --max-turns 100
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
          DATAFORSEO_LOGIN: ${{ secrets.DATAFORSEO_LOGIN }}
          DATAFORSEO_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.DATAFORSEO_PASSWORD }}
          MCP_TIMEOUT: "120000"

# (or via workflow_dispatch for testing). No schedule on purpose - the owner
# decides when to sweep. The scan reads the niche for the SUBJECTS being
# talked about RIGHT NOW - launches, Reddit/HN buzz, Google Trends - and
# puts them on the radar via propose_trend_topic. It proposes NO guide ideas
# and runs NO SERP checks: stage 2 (seo-trend-expand.yml) does that, only

jobs:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    permissions:

            exit 1
          fi
          case "$CLAUDE_TOKEN" in
            sk-ant-oat*) echo "Claude token present." ;;
            *)
              echo "::error::CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN does not look like a Claude Code OAuth token (expected it to start with sk-ant-oat). It was probably line-wrapped or the wrong text was pasted when it was saved. Rerun the setup command from your DispatchSEO dashboard to mint and verify a replacement."
              exit 1 ;;
          esac

      # Fail loudly if the seo-manager MCP is unreachable. Without this, a
      # stale URL in mcp-ci.json makes Claude see an empty toolset and "exit
      # cleanly" - a green run that scanned nothing (2026-07-14 domain-move bug).
      - name: Preflight - seo-manager MCP reachable

        run: |

          code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 30 -X POST "$url" \

      - name: Run the trend scan
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          # Full transcript in the step log so a run's evidence (what hype was
          # seen, what made the radar) stays readable after the fact.
          show_full_output: true
          prompt: "FIRST call the seo-manager MCP tool get_instructions with workflow trend-scan, then follow the returned markdown exactly - it is the current playbook and overrides any cached knowledge of this pipeline. Also read .dispatchseo/conventions.md for what this product is. In brief: dismiss stale radar topics, sweep the niche's launches / Reddit and HN buzz / Google Trends (dataforseo MCP where available) for the SUBJECTS being talked about right now, then put at most 5 on the radar via propose_trend_topic with the evidence attached. Do NOT propose suggestions and do NOT run SERP checks - the owner picks subjects on the dashboard and a separate expand run turns picks into guide ideas.           claude_args: |

            --permission-mode bypassPermissions
            --max-turns 100
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          SEO_MCP_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.SEO_MCP_API_KEY }}
          DATAFORSEO_LOGIN: ${{ secrets.DATAFORSEO_LOGIN }}

          MCP_TIMEOUT: "120000"
